sanskrit:
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः।
तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम॥
transliteration:
yatra yogeśvaraḥ kṛṣṇo yatra pārtho dhanur-dharaḥ |
tatra śrīr vijayo bhūtir dhruvā nītir matir mama ||
literal_meaning: Wherever Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, is, and wherever Arjuna, the bow-bearer, is—there will certainly be prosperity, victory, power, and steadfast morality: this is my conviction.
contextual_meaning: The psyche that aligns conscious skill (Arjuna) with the trans-personal Self (Krishna) becomes an unstoppable system; inner conflict dissolves into coherent action, and external success is a by-product of inner integration.
psychological_state: Post-therapy consolidation: the client has internalized both the compassionate inner mentor (Krishna) and the capable ego (Arjuna), so anxiety drops and decisive action feels natural.
trigger_scenario: You walk into the job interview, the exam hall, or the difficult conversation, and instead of rehearsing lines you quietly sense two inner voices aligned—your calm wisdom and your trained competence—so you speak spontaneously and things go better than expected.
anchor_text: Align your skills with higher purpose; success follows inner unity.
